reforms.description: The Chilean government is implementing a comprehensive Productivity Agenda that includes 47 measures aimed at boosting innovation and productivity across various sectors. This agenda fosters public-private coordination through new Strategic Programmes to promote the emergence of innovation clusters and proposes institutional changes to improve coordination among innovation policymaking agencies, including the establishment of a Ministry of Innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The measures are designed to enhance innovation and productivity, which are key drivers of economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The agenda is currently being implemented with various measures already in place.
reforms.importance_rationale: The comprehensive nature of the agenda and its structural changes signify a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: A new effort is being devoted to Strategic Programmes (Programas Estratégicos), which foster public-private coordination in potentially high growth sectors.

reforms.description: The Chilean government announced the creation of a Productivity Commission to analyze and recommend policies aimed at stimulating productivity across various sectors. Additionally, pilot evaluations of 1 in 10 of its innovation programmes are being carried out, with plans for comprehensive implementation in 2016 to strengthen the innovation system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the commission and the evaluation of innovation programmes are expected to enhance productivity and innovation capabilities.
reforms.status_evidence: The commission has been announced, and pilot evaluations are in progress.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these reforms are part of a broader agenda and do not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently carrying out pilot evaluations of 1 in 10 of the programmes, and plans to begin a comprehensive implementation in 2016.

reforms.description: New programs were launched in 2014 and 2015 to enhance linkages between universities and companies, including an innovation voucher and an R&D firm-university contract instrument. Additionally, the Ministry of Economy and CORFO launched a Strategic Programme on Intelligent Specialization in 2015, focusing on public-private coordination in key sectors to identify gaps and propose technological roadmaps.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to strengthen collaboration between academia and industry, fostering innovation and addressing sector-specific needs.
reforms.status_evidence: The programs have been launched and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programs enhance existing frameworks but do not represent a fundamental shift in policy.
reforms.source_quote: In 2014 and 2015 new programmes aimed at increasing the linkage between universities and companies were launched: an innovation voucher and a R&D firm-university contract instrument.

reforms.description: Chile aims to promote more participation of women in engineering and computer sciences to address the lack of advanced human capital in STEM fields, which is a significant barrier to productivity improvements for firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing participation of women in STEM is expected to enhance the talent pool and drive productivity improvements.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important, this initiative is part of broader efforts to improve human capital and does not represent a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Promote more women participation in the fields of engineering and computer sciences.
